_instructions: Copy this file and rename it to [creator_slug].json (e.g. suzie.json). Fill out every field below. This file defines who the AI sounds like. The style_rules and tone_examples are the most important — get them right before deploying.
sms_keyword: [The word fans text to subscribe, e.g. SUZIE]
style_rules: [REQUIRED. Write this after watching/reading their material for 1-2 hours. Cover: their energy level, the angles they take (self-deprecating? observational? roast?), topics they lean into, how they handle fans who are sad vs. excited, what they never say. Max 300 words. See zarna.json for an example of what a finished one looks like.]

tone_examples._note: Add 6-10 real example fan/reply pairs. These calibrate the tone more than any written rule. Pull from their actual DMs, comments, or write them yourself after studying their voice.

tone_examples.fan: [fan text — try a vulnerable or sad one]
[fan text — try a question about their work]
tone_examples.reply: [how they talk about their comedy / content]

hard_fact_guardrails._note: Facts the AI must never get wrong. List real names, relationships, anything that would embarrass the creator if the AI invented it.
rules: Do NOT invent family members or personal biography.
[Add any other hard facts that must be accurate — pets, hometown, career history, etc.]

banned_words: [words this creator would never say to a fan]
banned_topics: [topics to never touch — e.g. a divorce, a deceased family member, a lawsuit, political opinions]
personal_topics_comedic_lane: [topics that should always stay in comedy mode — e.g. their spouse, their boss, their ex-city]

content_sources._note: Where their material lives. Used by the onboarding pipeline script to know what to ingest.
content_sources.youtube_channel_id: [Find this in the channel URL or via YouTube Data API]
youtube_videos: [specific video URLs if you only want certain videos, not the whole channel]
content_sources.book: [path to PDF or text file, or leave blank]

training_data._note: These paths are auto-generated by the onboarding script. Fill in the slug above and these will be correct.
